trigger_reason: R744 is rank 3 by mechanical-PASS proximity in E30 (kw=2, tree-stream max sim=0.50, q-rubric=0.40). Per program_v10.md §2.5 (preserved in v11), top-3 rounds receive full step-13 spec.
candidate_stripped: Layer-depth controller that uses E_2-page convergence of a depth-filtered spectral sequence as a stopping criterion for layer expansion during progressive training.

base_model.name: Small transformer (6 layers max, 256 hidden, 4 heads) trained from scratch

A_candidate.name: E_2-page convergence depth controller (R744)
A_candidate.description: Train progressively: start at depth 1; every 200 steps, compute E_2 page differentials (changes in per-layer-prefix loss derivatives); if all differentials < ε=0.01 for last 3 checkpoints, freeze depth; else add a layer up to max 6. Final depth determined by E_2 convergence test.
A_candidate.distinctive_parameter: Depth-add trigger based on E_2-page differential threshold (algebraic-topological criterion)
A_candidate.expected_behavior: If E_2 convergence captures meaningful depth-vs-information criterion, A reaches final perplexity at lower depth than B or matches at same depth.

B_control.name: Loss-derivative depth controller (no E_2 formalism)
B_control.description: Same progressive setup but use the simpler criterion: if per-layer-marginal loss reduction < 0.01 for last 3 checkpoints, freeze depth. No E_2 page; just direct derivative threshold.
B_control.distinctive_parameter_value: Direct loss-derivative threshold (= E_1 page only, no E_2 differentials)
B_control.expected_behavior: Null hypothesis: A's E_2 calculation collapses to B's E_1 derivative threshold (the E_2 page often equals E_1 for short filtrations).

C_baseline.name: Fixed-depth baseline (full 6 layers, no progressive)
C_baseline.description: Train standard 6-layer model from scratch for 1K steps; no depth control.

metric.name: WikiText-103 perplexity at end of training + final depth selected
metric.measurement_protocol: Train each variant for 1K steps; record final perplexity and (for A/B) final depth. 3 seeds.

statistical_test.name: Paired t-test A vs B on perplexity, A vs B on final depth, A vs C on perplexity
statistical_test.expected_outcome_if_candidate_has_value: A reaches similar perplexity as B at LOWER final depth (E_2 detects convergence earlier) OR A reaches lower perplexity at same depth.
statistical_test.expected_outcome_if_candidate_collapses_: A and B select the same final depth; perplexity indistinguishable.

distinguishability_pre_check.rationale: E_2 page differentials ARE mathematically distinct from E_1 derivatives — they incorporate the SECOND-order structure (changes in changes). For a filtration of depth 6 with non-trivial cross-layer interactions, E_2 may differ from E_1. However, for short filtrations (depth ≤ 6) and small models (~6M params), the E_2 page often agrees with E_1 because the higher differentials require pages r > 2 to differ. The empirical distinguishability is plausible but not guaranteed.
distinguishability_pre_check.implication: Worth running. If A ≈ B in final depth and perplexity, R744 collapses to direct-derivative depth control (analogous to layer-importance-derivative methods in NAS).

notes_for_executor: E_2 page differential computation: maintain partial losses L_0,...,L_d; compute first differences d_1[k] = L_k - L_{k-1} and second differences d_2[k] = d_1[k] - d_1[k-1]. E_2 convergence = max |d_2| < ε.
